hypothesis: Reproduce poor hard-ST phase-5 anchor on this machine before new phase-6 exploration.
notes: Confirm fq_acc=0.2436, fq_route=0.0034, fq_exit=41.48; test close to phase-5 anchor.

hypothesis: Reproduce medium-basin ES rescue phase-5 anchor on this machine before new phase-6 exploration.
notes: Test/confirm both remain perfect on overall accuracy, final-query accuracy, route match, and exit time.

hypothesis: Reproduce weak-basin ES partial-rescue phase-5 anchor on this machine before new phase-6 exploration.
notes: Reproduced weak-basin partial rescue: perfect routing with partial task recovery.

hypothesis: Weak-basin ES rescue plus frozen-routing content refinement should recover task accuracy without breaking route timing.
notes: Test overall=0.9225, fq_acc=0.8636, fq_route=0.8155, fq_exit=114.75. Better task accuracy than weak anchor, but route timing slipped.

hypothesis: Factorized WAIT/ACT controller should improve final-query discovery versus flat router2 baseline.
notes: Initial scout only received ~51 resumed updates before the train_steps_delta fix; do not compare it against later phase-6 runs.

hypothesis: Factorized WAIT/ACT controller should improve final-query discovery versus flat router2 baseline.
notes: Test overall=0.4590, route_match=0.1960, delay_rate=0.4779, fq_acc=0.2650, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=3.92. Learns to delay in aggregate but still exits almost immediately on final-query cases.

hypothesis: Stronger control-state plus larger packet memory should preserve needs_final_query longer.
notes: Initial memoryheavy scout hit checkpoint shape-mismatch when packet-memory slot count changed; superseded by seed985_p2 after load_checkpoint(strict=False) filtering fix.

hypothesis: Stronger control-state plus larger packet memory should preserve needs_final_query longer.
notes: Test overall=0.6325, route_match=0.5104, delay_rate=0.7026, fq_acc=0.2500, fq_route=0.0007, fq_exit=33.04. Best corrected discovery scout so far on overall accuracy and exit timing, but final-query route discovery is still essentially absent.

hypothesis: Extra wait supervision should stabilize factorized controller discovery before release.
notes: Test overall=0.4519, route_match=0.2234, delay_rate=0.4955, fq_acc=0.2400, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=4.95. Heavier wait CE alone does not fix final-query release timing.

hypothesis: Weak-basin ES rescue plus frozen-routing content refinement should recover task accuracy without breaking route timing.
notes: Corrected rerun held up: test overall=0.9105, fq_acc=0.8529, fq_route=0.8128, fq_exit=114.51. Slightly lower than p1 on task/route, but same qualitative improvement over the weak ES anchor.

hypothesis: Short oracle-forced route imitation followed by hard release may discover a usable final-query waiting policy from scratch.
notes: Best discovery scout so far: test overall=0.6406, route_match=0.5290, fq_acc=0.3229, fq_route=0.1096, fq_exit=74.96. First corrected phase-6 run with meaningful final-query route recovery and exit timing far later than the B/C scouts.

hypothesis: Controller-only imitation-release might stabilize discovery by isolating the routing module from content drift.
notes: Weaker than full-model imitation-release: test overall=0.5124, route_match=0.3590, fq_acc=0.2373, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=20.25. Longer controller-only release did not reproduce the D1 gain.

hypothesis: Adding a recurrent wait-state on top of memoryheavy control should preserve and expose the long-lived wait signal better than control-state alone.
notes: Wait-state retention underperformed the simpler memoryheavy variant: test overall=0.5332, route_match=0.3815, fq_acc=0.2420, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=22.00. Added wait-state did not convert the retained signal into a better final-query policy.

hypothesis: Exact rerun of the first promising imitation-release discovery config before promotion.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the original D1 discovery result: test overall=0.6406, route_match=0.5290, fq_acc=0.3229, fq_route=0.1096, fq_exit=74.96.

hypothesis: Longer imitation-release plus route-match selection might extend the useful final-query waiting policy beyond the original D1 schedule.
notes: Tuned D variant regressed sharply: test overall=0.4512, route_match=0.2773, fq_acc=0.2393, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=4.77. Longer release plus route-match selection did not preserve the D1 discovery effect.

hypothesis: Direct release-gate supervision may stop the factorized WAIT/ACT controller from exiting too early on final-query cases.
notes: Partial B improvement only: test overall=0.6150, route_match=0.4463, delay_rate=0.6902, fq_acc=0.2735, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=29.58. It pushes final-query cases later than the scout, but still fails to discover the correct final-query route.

hypothesis: Release-specific auxiliary supervision may help the factorized controller preserve a wait state and learn when to release.
notes: Release auxiliary alone stayed weak: test overall=0.4524, route_match=0.1770, delay_rate=0.3632, fq_acc=0.2590, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=1.84. It did not stop the factorized controller from immediately dumping final-query cases.

hypothesis: Selecting on final-query exit time and keeping the memoryheavy control path may push retained control state into a later and more useful release policy.
notes: Best C tuning result so far on final-query routing: test overall=0.5902, route_match=0.6006, delay_rate=0.7094, fq_acc=0.2420, fq_route=0.1979, fq_exit=53.73. It still misses on task accuracy, but exit-time selection finally produced non-trivial final-query route discovery.

hypothesis: Limiting weak-basin content refinement to memory, packet adapter, and readout may preserve the rescued route while improving task quality more efficiently than refining the whole content stack.
notes: Memory/readout-only recovery stayed useful but clearly below refine-all: test overall=0.7842, route_match=0.7090, fq_acc=0.6243, fq_route=0.4973, fq_exit=95.70. Weak-basin rescue still appears to need more than memory/readout alone.

hypothesis: Longer EXIT masking and slower route-release decay may convert the later final-query waiting behavior into real route discovery for the factorized controller.
notes: Unlock-late was negative: test overall=0.4678, route_match=0.2009, fq_acc=0.2635, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=6.62. Longer exit masks did not turn the factorized controller's later waiting into final-query route discovery.

hypothesis: Re-opening the packet/state core along with memory and readout may recover most of refine-all performance while still freezing routing after ES rescue.
notes: Best A local-tuning result so far: test overall=0.9274, route_match=0.8981, fq_acc=0.8777, fq_route=0.8369, fq_exit=116.85. Stronger than memory/readout and slightly ahead of refine-all on this seed, so it needs an exact rerun before promotion.

hypothesis: Exact rerun of the first C variant with non-trivial final-query route discovery before deciding on promotion.
notes: Exact rerun matched the original C result numerically: test overall=0.5902, route_match=0.6006, fq_acc=0.2420, fq_route=0.1979, fq_exit=53.73. This family is real enough to promote as a retention/control candidate, even though task accuracy is still poor.

hypothesis: Exact rerun of the strongest weak-basin sinkcore refinement before promotion to seed panels and confirmation.
notes: Exact rerun matched p1 numerically: test overall=0.9274, route_match=0.8981, fq_acc=0.8777, fq_route=0.8369, fq_exit=116.85. Sinkcore is now a verified promoted A candidate alongside refine-all.

hypothesis: Selecting on final-query exit time while keeping the best imitation-release discovery schedule may convert the D1 partial discovery effect into a later and more correct release policy.
notes: Exit-time selection did not preserve the D1 discovery effect: test overall=0.6351, route_match=0.5046, fq_acc=0.2607, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=33.91. It retained aggregate delay behavior but collapsed back to premature final-query release.

hypothesis: Supervising only the persistent control target may stabilize the factorized wait controller better than heavier route or release losses that overfit the full oracle route.
notes: Control-only supervision stayed in the same weak regime as the other F variants: test overall=0.4680, route_match=0.2075, fq_acc=0.2630, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=6.27. It did not convert the factorized controller's aggregate delay behavior into a correct final-query release policy.

hypothesis: Adding a dedicated recurrent control cell to the wait/act controller may preserve a discoverable wait policy longer than the flat factorized controller variants.
notes: Recurrent control cell stayed negative on the key metric: test overall=0.4978, route_match=0.2864, fq_acc=0.2610, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=16.22. It delays more than immediate-exit collapse but still fails to discover the final-query route.

hypothesis: Sticky control state plus larger packet memory may preserve the needs_final_query signal more durably than the set/clear memoryheavy variants.
notes: Sticky control without exit-time tuning was weaker than the set/clear family: test overall=0.5446, route_match=0.4043, fq_acc=0.2320, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=23.55. It delays more than immediate collapse, but does not discover the final-query route.

hypothesis: Moving the set/clear mechanism into a simpler wait/act controller may clarify whether the control-state abstraction helps even without the heavier controlwaitact routing stack.
notes: Final unrun B scaffold was also negative: test overall=0.4929, route_match=0.3293, fq_acc=0.2440, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=14.77. This closes the initial B-family sweep with no variant achieving non-trivial final-query route discovery.

hypothesis: Adding extra control-input scale and a more persistent write bias may turn sticky-control memory into a usable long-horizon keepalive signal.
notes: Best sticky-control result so far and the first sticky variant with real final-query routing: test overall=0.5472, route_match=0.5960, fq_acc=0.3349, fq_route=0.4472, fq_exit=93.57, delay_rate=0.8287. Task accuracy is still modest, but keepalive clearly changed the long-horizon control behavior and deserves an exact rerun.

hypothesis: Pretraining the controller on final-query-only cases may isolate the discovery problem enough to learn a reusable wait-until-query policy before returning to the mixed benchmark.
notes: Finalquery-only controller pretraining was a decisive negative: test overall=0.2396, route_match=0.0, fq_acc=0.2396, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=3.49. Isolating the mode alone did not teach a reusable wait-until-query policy.

hypothesis: Adapter-only weak-basin refinement may preserve the rescued route while giving a cheaper and potentially more stable recovery path than refine-all or sinkcore.
notes: Adapter-only recovery was useful but not a new A leader: test overall=0.7555, route_match=0.6803, fq_acc=0.5642, fq_route=0.4352, fq_exit=90.90. It recovers the weak basin substantially, but remains below memory/readout, refine-all, and sinkcore.

hypothesis: Exact rerun of the first sticky-control keepalive result before promotion or confirmation.
notes: Exact rerun matched the original keepalive result numerically: test overall=0.5472, route_match=0.5960, fq_acc=0.3349, fq_route=0.4472, fq_exit=93.57. Sticky keepalive is now a verified C candidate rather than a one-off.

hypothesis: Running the plain router2 setclear baseline under the phase-6 bookkeeping gives a fair anchor against the more specialized discovery variants and closes the initial dev config surface.
notes: Plain router2/setclear baseline is weak inside the same phase-6 setup: test overall=0.4580, route_match=0.2676, fq_acc=0.2620, fq_route=0.0, fq_exit=2.87. This closes the initial phase-6 dev config surface with a clean baseline anchor.

Exact same-seed rerun matched the stronger memoryheavy-exitselect memoryreadout bridge numerically: test overall=0.7354, route_match=0.6517, fq_acc=0.4586, fq_route=0.2894, fq_exit=61.98. Lighter reopening is now a verified improvement over sinkcore for this weaker C-family source.

Adapter-only keepalive reopening is viable but clearly weaker than memoryreadout/sinkcore: trainer test overall=0.8652, route_match=0.7992, fq_acc=0.8082, fq_route=0.7313, fq_exit=127.00. Independent verification stayed close on test (0.8626/0.8034/0.7955/0.7253) and confirm (0.8606/0.8096/0.7918/0.7332).

Exact same-seed rerun matched the adapter-only keepalive bridge numerically: test overall=0.8652, route_match=0.7992, fq_acc=0.8082, fq_route=0.7313, fq_exit=127.00. This is now a verified intermediate reopening regime.

hypothesis: Paired comparison against sinkcore and memoryreadout on the weaker memoryheavy-exitselect source: if adapter-only reopening is enough, most of this family's gain comes from preserving route timing with minimal content correction.
notes: Adapter-only reopening on the weaker memoryheavy-exitselect source landed in a real tradeoff regime: trainer test overall=0.6637, route_match=0.6631, fq_acc=0.3115, fq_route=0.3108, fq_exit=64.17. Independent verification stayed close on test (0.6738/0.6745/0.3322/0.3336/66.99) and confirm (0.6479/0.6565/0.3041/0.3211/65.06).

hypothesis: Paired comparison against sinkcore and memoryreadout on the weaker forceoracle source: if adapter-only reopening is enough, most of the residual error is readout-level rather than broader content repair.
retirement_reason: Dominated by memoryreadout on the same reproduced source checkpoint.
notes: Forceoracle adapter-only reopening is slightly worse than memoryreadout across the board: test overall=0.6423, route_match=0.5316, fq_acc=0.3309, fq_route=0.1230, fq_exit=75.18. No rerun queued because it is cleanly dominated by the lighter memoryreadout bridge.

hypothesis: Exact rerun of the weaker-family adapter tradeoff point to determine whether its surprisingly higher final-query route match than memoryreadout is real or just seed noise.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the weaker-family adapter tradeoff point numerically: test overall=0.6637, route_match=0.6631, fq_acc=0.3115, fq_route=0.3108, fq_exit=64.17. This is now a verified boundary point below memoryreadout on task accuracy but with slightly higher final-query route match.

hypothesis: If the near-perfect keepalive bridge mostly needs memory plus sink/readout repair, dropping packet_adapter should still preserve most of the long-horizon recovery effect.
notes: Strong-basin ablation stayed near the keepalive memoryreadout bridge even without packet_adapter: trainer test overall=0.9561, route_match=0.9268, fq_acc=0.9753, fq_route=0.9659, fq_exit=125.41. Independent verification stayed close on test (0.9567/0.9290/0.9766/0.9679/125.79) and confirm (0.9565/0.9307/0.9685/0.9559/125.09).

hypothesis: Exact rerun of the strong-basin memorysinkreadout ablation to determine whether dropping packet_adapter still reproduces the near-perfect keepalive bridge.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the memorysinkreadout keepalive bridge numerically: test overall=0.9561, route_match=0.9268, fq_acc=0.9753, fq_route=0.9659, fq_exit=125.41. This is now a verified result: reopened memory plus sink/readout is enough; packet_adapter is not essential in the strong keepalive basin.

hypothesis: If the strong keepalive bridge mostly needs sink/readout correction and route preservation, readout-only reopening should retain more of the long-horizon gain than expected; if it collapses, reopened memory is the crucial ingredient.
notes: Minimal-opening ablation remained viable but clearly weaker than memorysinkreadout: trainer test overall=0.8571, route_match=0.7900, fq_acc=0.7914, fq_route=0.7126, fq_exit=127.00. Independent verification stayed close on test (0.8633/0.7933/0.7928/0.7052/127.00) and confirm (0.8606/0.8003/0.7855/0.7148/126.90).

hypothesis: Exact rerun of the strong-basin readout-only ablation to determine whether nearly all of the keepalive bridge can survive without reopening memory at all.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the readout-only keepalive ablation numerically: test overall=0.8571, route_match=0.7900, fq_acc=0.7914, fq_route=0.7126, fq_exit=127.00. Readout-only is a stable but clearly weaker regime than reopening memory.

hypothesis: If sink_proj is not a major part of the strong keepalive recovery, reopening memory plus readout without sink_proj should remain close to the memorysinkreadout result.
notes: Strong-basin isolation run stayed near-perfect and verification matched closely: trainer test overall=0.9596, route_match=0.9258, fq_acc=0.9793, fq_route=0.9706, fq_exit=125.92. Independent verification stayed close on test (0.9544/0.9225/0.9746/0.9652/125.90) and confirm (0.9600/0.9329/0.9748/0.9661/125.59).

hypothesis: If fixed sink/readout are already adequate once memory is reopened, memory-only refinement should preserve a meaningful share of the strong keepalive bridge.
notes: Strong-basin isolation run showed reopened memory alone was enough: trainer test overall=0.9613, route_match=0.9323, fq_acc=0.9826, fq_route=0.9739, fq_exit=126.13. Independent verification stayed close on test (0.9567/0.9229/0.9753/0.9659/125.93) and confirm (0.9641/0.9348/0.9777/0.9700/125.75).

hypothesis: Exact rerun of the strong-basin memory_+readout-without-sink ablation to test whether sink_proj is truly unnecessary.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the memoryreadout-nosink keepalive bridge numerically: test overall=0.9596, route_match=0.9258, fq_acc=0.9793, fq_route=0.9706, fq_exit=125.92. sink_proj is not required in the strong keepalive basin.

hypothesis: Exact rerun of the strong-basin memory-only ablation to determine whether reopened memory alone is enough in the keepalive basin.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the memory-only keepalive bridge numerically: test overall=0.9613, route_match=0.9323, fq_acc=0.9826, fq_route=0.9739, fq_exit=126.13. reopened memory alone is enough for near-perfect recovery in the strong keepalive basin.
